g.data-narration: Welcome to this lesson on Standard Error - the key to understanding how reliable your sample data really is.
g.text: Standard Error: Estimating the Reliability of Your Sample

g.data-narration: We want to know about a whole population but can only study a small sample. How confident can we be in our results?
g.text: We collect sample data (e.g., salaries of 30 CS graduates) to estimate a
population parameter (average salary of ALL CS graduates).
Question: How much can we trust our sample mean as an estimate?

g.data-narration: Standard Error measures how much your sample statistic would vary if you took many different samples from the population.
g.text: The Standard Error (SE) is the estimated standard deviation of a
sampling distribution. It tells us how much sample statistics vary
from sample to sample due to random chance alone.

g.data-narration: The sampling distribution shows what happens when we take many samples. Its spread is measured by the Standard Error.
g.text: Imagine taking ALL possible samples of size n, calculating each mean,
and plotting them. This is the sampling distribution of the mean.

g.data-narration: The Standard Error of the Mean formula is SE equals sample standard deviation divided by square root of sample size.
g.text: Important: To HALVE the SE, you must QUADRUPLE the sample size!

g.data-narration: Two factors determine Standard Error: sample variability (s) and sample size (n). Both directly impact how precise our estimate is.
g.text: More variation in population → Larger SE → Less precise
Larger sample → Smaller SE → More precise (inverse √n)

g.data-narration: This graph shows how Standard Error decreases as sample size increases. Notice the inverse square root relationship clearly.
g.text: • To halve SE, multiply n by 4!

g.data-narration: Standard Error is essential for Confidence Intervals. The SE determines the margin of error and thus how wide our interval is.
g.text: A 95% CI for the population mean is constructed using the Standard Error:

g.data-narration: In hypothesis testing, the Standard Error appears in the denominator of test statistics, determining if our results are statistically significant.
g.text: In a t-test, the test statistic is calculated as:

g.data-narration: Let's calculate the Standard Error for CS graduate salaries. With s=2 Lakhs and n=30, SE equals approximately 36,514 Rupees.
g.text: Interpretation: Sample means would vary from true mean by ~₹36,514 on average

g.data-narration: With this Standard Error, we can now build a 95% confidence interval of 7.75 to 9.24 Lakhs for the true average package.
g.text: We're 95% confident the true mean lies here!

g.data-narration: Remember: Standard Deviation measures variation WITHIN your sample, while Standard Error measures uncertainty in your sample MEAN.
g.text: Standard Error vs Standard Deviation - Know the Difference!
Uncertainty in the SAMPLE MEAN estimate
"s tells you spread of data" | "SE tells you precision of mean estimate"

g.data-narration: Standard Error is essential for data science, A/B testing, and machine learning. It quantifies confidence in any estimate from sample data.
g.text: Standard Error: Your Quantifiable Measure of Confidence in Sample Data
